metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a DET branch to `_descriptor_from_prompt` that skips `get_prompt` for `exec: DET` criteria and constructs the descriptor from routing metadata only (per the plan's specified fallback shape: `name` from routing or id, `scenario` from detector envelope `message`, `facet` from routing or id, `checklist` empty).
findings.finding: `_descriptor_from_prompt` unconditionally calls `prompts.get_prompt(f'plan-review-{cid}', ...)` with no DET branch; a `project.X` criterion with `exec: DET` and no `.md` rubric file causes `load_criteria` to raise `RegistryError`, aborting the entire criteria load. The plan specifies adding a DET branch that skips `get_prompt`, but no such branch exists in the codebase.
findings.location: §Overlay schema — 'DET fallback descriptor shape' / `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:344`
findings.suggested_fix: In `_descriptor_from_prompt`, check `routing.get('exec','').upper() == 'DET'` before calling `get_prompt`; if true, build the descriptor from routing keys alone, resolving `scenario` from the detector envelope at `.rebar/detectors/<id>` if present.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify or specify how a plan-review LLM criterion runs against an inline fixture without the `eval_solver.case_store` git/store scaffolding — either confirm that `pass1.run_criterion(criterion_id, plan_text, runner=...)` (or equivalent) is the intended reuse path, or document the new execution seam the preview endpoint and `rebar criteria eval` CLI will use, so the 'reuses the per-criterion eval runner' claim is grounded.
findings.finding: The plan claims the editor live-preview endpoint (`POST /criterion/preview`) 'reuses the per-criterion eval runner behind the `agents` extra + credentials,' but `eval_solver.run_case` — the only existing criterion-level eval runner — requires a disposable git repo + seeded rebar store per case (see `eval_solver.py:55-112`), which is architecturally incompatible with the inline `{criterion_id | inline(prompt+routing), fixture}` body shape the preview endpoint describes. The plan offers no evidence that a plan-review LLM criterion can be driven synchronously against an inline fixture without the full `case_store` scaffolding.
findings.location: § 'What exists vs. what we build' → 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview' bullet; also AC line `rebar criteria eval <id>`
findings.suggested_fix: If the plan-review Pass-1 runner (`pass1.py` or `passes.py`) already accepts an inline plan string + runner injection, name that as the reuse seam rather than `eval_solver`. If no such seam exists, add it to scope explicitly rather than presenting the preview as a reuse story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and implement the dispatch decision rule for `exec: DET` criteria at the `exec_tier` / `_project_criteria` / `run_pass1` boundary — either add a `DET` return value to `exec_tier` and a third arm in `ProductionBatchRunner._resolve_criteria`, or route DET project criteria through a separate `proj_det` list that feeds the generalized `engine_b.scan` consumer rather than `run_pass1`'s LLM pool.
findings.finding: `exec_tier()` has no `DET` branch — it returns `1-TURN` for any criterion with `exec: DET` — so a project DET criterion flowing through `_project_criteria → route_criteria → exec_tier` lands in the LLM single-turn chunk pool (`proj_single`) rather than being routed to the net-new DET scan consumer. The plan describes DET scan consumer dispatch as net-new but never specifies the decision rule that separates `exec: DET` from LLM tiers at the `exec_tier`/`run_pass1`/`_project_criteria` boundary.
findings.location: §What exists vs. what we build — 'Generalize the DET-scan consumer' / `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:519-525`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `fire`/`no-fire` verdict derivation rule for the `POST /criterion/preview` endpoint (e.g., 'fire = at least one finding returned for LLM criteria; fire = at least one match for DET criteria') and add a response schema file, co-located with the endpoint spec.
findings.finding: The `POST /criterion/preview` endpoint output enum `{verdict: 'fire'|'no-fire'}` is defined only in the plan's prose. No schema file exists for this shape, and the derivation protocol — how an arbitrary LLM criterion's raw findings output (a list of finding dicts) maps to the `fire` / `no-fire` binary verdict — is never specified. The existing `eval_solver.run_case` returns domain-specific output shapes per hard-coded `prompt_id` (e.g. `completion.verify_completion`, `operations.review_ticket`), not a normalized fire/no-fire verdict; the plan's 'criterion-aware runner' is net-new but its output schema and decision rule are absent.
findings.location: §What exists vs. what we build — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC to ef7e (or 7f0d) that a blocking project LLM criterion whose call errors or times out is treated as skip=PASS (fail-open), with a test asserting this behavior.
findings.finding: No child's Acceptance Criteria explicitly covers the parent requirement that a blocking LLM criterion whose call errors or times out must fail open (skip, not block). The parent design states this as a distinct net-new behavior ('LLM fail-open: same rule as DET'), but ef7e's ACs address running LLM criteria and cache isolation, not error-path posture; 7f0d's ACs address DET fail_mode only.
findings.location: child ef7e-c3ed-296b-4642 (and absence across all siblings)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to ef7e's AC list: '[ ] A project LLM criterion configured to block whose LLM call errors or times out fails open (verdict=skip, does not block); a unit test asserts this using a mock that raises/times out.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the async job+poll fallback shape for `POST /criterion/preview` (at minimum: the polling endpoint path, the in-process job store type, the timeout/expiry policy, and how job results are cleared) so the spike gate has a concrete fallback path, not just a label.
findings.finding: The plan's editor live-preview endpoint (`POST /criterion/preview`) includes a spike gate: 'if a single sync preview does not return within the timeout on the reference model, fall back to an async job+poll shape rather than blocking the editor's `ThreadingHTTPServer` handler.' The fallback mechanism is unspecified. The shipped `ThreadingHTTPServer` editor (editor.py:612-614) exposes exactly four POST paths and has no job registry, no in-process async-job store, and no polling endpoint. The async fallback would require at minimum a new polling endpoint (e.g. `/criterion/preview/status?job=<id>`) and an in-process job dictionary — neither is designed in the plan. An implementer who hits the timeout path has no spec to build from.
findings.location: §Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview; src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py:612-614 (the current POST path whitelist)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the LLM runner (requiring the `agents` extra + credentials) is instantiated inside the editor's `ThreadingHTTPServer` handler for `POST /criterion/preview` (e.g. per-request `get_runner(cfg)`, a session-scoped singleton, or a background thread pool), and if the spike gate fires, design the concrete async job+poll fallback (job registry, poll endpoint, timeout cleanup) before committing to the sync-first approach.
findings.finding: The `POST /criterion/preview` endpoint is described as 'reusing the per-criterion eval runner behind the `agents` extra + credentials', but the existing editor `ThreadingHTTPServer` has no LLM runner injection point, no credential-resolution path, and no reference to the `agents` optional extra. How the live LLM runner is instantiated inside a `BaseHTTPRequestHandler.do_POST` call — where `get_runner(cfg)` may need env vars, the `agents` extra installed, and a running event loop — is unspecified. The spike gate correctly identifies the timeout risk but the fallback 'async job+poll shape' is also unspecified: `ThreadingHTTPServer` has no built-in job queue, and adding one (thread pool, in-process dict of futures) is non-trivial state management in a stdlib HTTP handler.
findings.location: § Net-new (build): 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview — a new synchronous editor endpoint POST /criterion/preview … reusing the per-criterion eval runner behind the `agents` extra + credentials, with a configurable timeout (default 60s) … Spike gate: if a single sync preview does not return within the timeout … fall back to an async job+poll shape'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a slip-handling policy for the b744 dependency in AC 7: either (a) define a time-box after which AC 7 is descoped to a follow-on child ticket, or (b) create an explicit child story for the post-b744 unification slice so this epic can close on its own ACs independently.
findings.finding: AC 7 ('A shared rebar.llm.criteria layer that both plan-review and code-review consume') is gated on b744 landing, but b744 has an open dependency (WS6 = Gerrit voter pending) with no stated slip-handling — if b744 slips indefinitely, this epic's AC 7 remains permanently open, leaving the epic in an indeterminate done-state.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC 7; Build order step 5; Dependency section
findings.suggested_fix: Split AC 7 into a standalone child story ('Unify plan-review + code-review registries post-b744') that is explicitly blocked on b744, and remove it from this epic's ACs. The epic then closes when ACs 1–6 + 8–9 are done; the child story closes when b744 lands.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in §Attestation that the `registry_version(repo_root)` overlay-hashing is implemented in story `08af` (not yet landed), and that attestations signed between story ef7e and 08af will not be invalidated by overlay edits; add a sequencing note so implementers know the overlay-invalidation invariant is not yet end-to-end.
findings.finding: The plan's §Attestation section specifies that `registry_version(repo_root=None)` will hash the `.rebar/` overlay when `repo_root` is given, ensuring an overlay edit invalidates prior attestations. But the shipped `attest.registry_version()` (attest.py:37-57) has no `repo_root` parameter and hashes only the packaged `_routing_index()` — never the overlay. The plan defers the fix to story `08af`, but §Attestation presents the mechanism as the current design rather than a forthcoming story. Concretely: during the window between story ef7e (MVP, DONE) and story 08af (step 6), an attestation signed against an overlay can be claimed against a DIFFERENT overlay — the overlay edit does NOT invalidate it. Additionally, `drift_refresh_candidate` and `remediation_mode_candidate` each call `registry_version()` without a `repo_root` arg (attest.py:278, 364), and the plan says to thread `repo_root` through them, but `registry_version` itself must first gain the param before the threading is meaningful. The plan conflates these two steps.
findings.location: §Attestation & invalidation (clears G6/COH/E1; reuses kind-keyed attest infra); src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:37-57 (registry_version signature), attest.py:278, attest.py:364 (callers)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to §Attestation: '(Implemented in story 08af — until that lands, `registry_version()` hashes the packaged routing only; overlay-editing does not invalidate prior attestations in the ef7e→08af window. Story 08af must add the `repo_root` param to `registry_version()` first, then thread it through `sign_plan_review`, `drift_refresh_candidate`, and `remediation_mode_candidate`.)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC verifying that editing/activating/disabling an overlay criterion invalidates a prior attestation at claim time (i.e., `registry_version(repo_root)` reflects overlay content, and a prior cert becomes stale rather than crashes), and that a disabled built-in appears in the `disabled_builtins` coverage field of the signed manifest.
findings.finding: The fully-described attestation-invalidation deliverable (§ Attestation & invalidation: registry_version(repo_root), threading repo_root through claim_gate_check/compute_validity/drift_refresh_candidate/remediation_mode_candidate, disabled_builtins coverage field) has no visible covering acceptance criterion.
findings.location: § Attestation & invalidation; Acceptance Criteria list (visible portion ends at 'rebar criteria eval <id>')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit ACs for (a) `registry_version(repo_root)` threading through `claim_gate_check`, `drift_refresh_candidate`, and `remediation_mode_candidate` with a RED test proving overlay edit invalidates a prior attestation, and (b) the `POST /criterion/preview` editor endpoint with a contract test covering timeout/fallback behavior.
findings.finding: Two significant non-deferred goals — (1) attestation invalidation via `registry_version(repo_root)` threading through `claim_gate_check → compute_validity`, `drift_refresh_candidate`, `remediation_mode_candidate`, and (2) editor live-preview endpoint (`POST /criterion/preview`) — appear in the body as committed scope but the AC list is truncated at the eval CLI entry and does not contain visible, end-state-observable acceptance criteria for either of these.
findings.location: § Attestation & invalidation; § What exists vs. what we build (editor live-preview); AC list (truncated at 'rebar criteria eval')
findings.suggested_fix: Append two ACs: (1) 'Editing `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` invalidates a live attestation at claim time: a test proves `claim_gate_check` returns stale when the overlay sha256 changes, covering `drift_refresh_candidate` and `remediation_mode_candidate` callers.' (2) '`POST /criterion/preview {criterion_id, fixture}` returns `{verdict, finding?, rationale}` within the configurable timeout; on timeout or LLM error it returns a defined fallback shape rather than hanging the `ThreadingHTTPServer` handler.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `POST /criterion/preview` to the docs deliverable — either extend ADR 0015 or add a dedicated editor-API section in CLAUDE.md — covering the request/response shape, the configurable timeout, and the spike-gate async job+poll fallback contract.
findings.finding: The new `POST /criterion/preview` editor endpoint — including its request/response contract, configurable timeout, and spike-gate async fallback (job+poll shape) — is introduced as net-new infrastructure but is not listed in the docs deliverable; the ADR and CLAUDE.md how-to focus on authoring/activating/eval'ing criteria, not on the editor HTTP API contract or its async fallback behavior.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview' subsection; § Migration & compatibility — Docs deliverable

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the async job+poll preview shape to the docs deliverable scope: if the spike gate fires and async is adopted, capture the decision (why sync was abandoned, the polling API contract, job lifecycle) in ADR 0015 or a companion ADR, and reference it from CLAUDE.md.
findings.finding: If the editor live-preview 'spike gate' fires and the async job+poll shape is adopted, the resulting architectural decision (async endpoint contract, job lifecycle, polling API) is not listed as a documentation deliverable in the ADR/CLAUDE.md update scope, leaving future maintainers without a record of why sync was abandoned and what the async contract is.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview' + § Migration & compatibility — 'Docs deliverable'
findings.suggested_fix: In the docs deliverable bullet, add: 'If the async job+poll fallback is adopted for the preview endpoint, extend ADR 0015 (or add ADR 0016) to document the polling contract, job lifecycle, and the spike-gate outcome that triggered the decision.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) define the async job+poll contract (endpoint paths, job-id schema, state enum, polling response shape, expiry semantics) as a conditional sub-spec within this epic, or (b) explicitly scope the spike to 'sync-only MVP; async is a follow-on story with its own spec' to remove the undefined fallback path.
findings.finding: The spike gate fallback ('fall back to an async job+poll shape') is referenced in the plan as an alternative to the synchronous `POST /criterion/preview` endpoint, but carries no contract: the async endpoint path, job-id schema, polling API, job lifecycle (queued/running/done/error states), and timeout/expiry semantics are all unspecified. If the spike fires, implementers have no spec to build against.
findings.location: §What exists vs. what we build — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview' / Spike gate paragraph

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit dependency on story #2 (7f0d) to 6e31's Scope section, noting that DET invariant preview requires the DET descriptor branch and generalized consumer from 7f0d.
findings.finding: 6e31's Acceptance Criteria require 'Authoring + preview work for both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant' but 6e31 only declares dependencies on story #1 (ef7e, overlay/activation) and story #3 (55b8, eval runner). DET invariant preview requires the DET fallback descriptor shape and the generalized DET consumer from 7f0d (#2) — without which a DET criterion has no descriptor and cannot be run through the preview eval runner.
findings.location: child 6e31-8846-0eee-404a
findings.suggested_fix: In 6e31's Scope, change 'Depends on story #1 (overlay/activation to author into) and story #3 (the runner the preview reuses)' to 'Depends on story #1 (overlay/activation), story #2 (7f0d — DET descriptor branch required for DET invariant preview), and story #3 (the runner the preview reuses)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC for the editor live-preview endpoint: `POST /criterion/preview` returns `{verdict, finding?, rationale}` within the configurable timeout (default 60s); on timeout breach the response follows the async job+poll shape; the endpoint requires the `agents` extra + credentials and is absent (404) without them.
findings.finding: The editor live-preview endpoint (`POST /criterion/preview`) is a described net-new deliverable with a spike-gate fallback and configurable timeout, but no visible acceptance criterion covers it.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview'; Build order step 4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Restore the full text of the `rebar criteria eval <id>` acceptance criterion (currently truncated) so the observable output, fixture behavior, and calibration-view requirements are stated completely.
findings.finding: The acceptance criterion for `rebar criteria eval <id>` is truncated mid-sentence ('must-fire/must-not- … [truncated by reconciler]'), leaving the full observable outcome undefined. An executor cannot determine what 'done' looks like for the per-criterion eval runner + calibration view AC.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `rebar criteria eval <id>` bullet (last visible AC)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or justify the dependency on story #2 (7f0d) in 08af's Scope; the attestation work (attest.py, registry_version, disabled_builtins) depends only on story #1 (ef7e overlay existence) and can proceed in parallel with 7f0d.
findings.finding: 08af declares a dependency on story #2 (7f0d, DET blocking generalization), but 08af's scope is exclusively attest.py, registry_version overlay hashing, and claim-gate threading. None of 08af's ACs require DET consumer generalization to be completed — disabled_builtins coverage depends only on the overlay (ef7e, #1) knowing which built-ins are disabled, and registry_version hashing is purely about reading overlay bytes. The spurious dependency could unnecessarily serialize 08af behind 7f0d, blocking attestation work that is independently implementable.
findings.location: child 08af-b2f0-aef0-4d59
findings.suggested_fix: In 08af's Scope, change 'Depends on story #1 (the overlay must exist to hash) and story #2 (DET blocking)' to 'Depends on story #1 (the overlay must exist to hash). Story #2 (7f0d) is independent and may proceed in parallel.'; add a note clarifying that disabled_builtins coverage requires only the overlay's disable entries, not the DET scan consumer.

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the missing step 6 to the build order (story `08af`: `registry_version(repo_root)` + attestation-invalidation threading through `claim_gate_check`, `drift_refresh_candidate`, `remediation_mode_candidate`), positioned before step 5 (b744 unification) since it is not blocked on b744.
findings.finding: Build order references 'story 08af, step 6 below' but the numbered build order only contains 5 steps (1–5); step 6 is never listed.
findings.location: § Build order — MVP slice note: 'The registry_version-reflects-the-overlay + attestation-invalidation half is split into story 08af, step 6 below'
findings.suggested_fix: Insert as step 2 or 3 (not blocked on b744): '**Attestation-invalidation (story `08af`):** `registry_version(repo_root)` hashes overlay; thread `repo_root` through `claim_gate_check → compute_validity`, `drift_refresh_candidate`, `remediation_mode_candidate`; `disabled_builtins` field in coverage dict; RED invalidation test.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either specify what the 'Routing derivation/validation pipeline' deliverable entails (design decision + AC) or explicitly mark it out-of-scope for this epic to remove the placeholder ambiguity.
findings.finding: The 'Routing derivation/validation pipeline' is listed in the Net-new (build) section but has no design detail, no story reference, and no corresponding acceptance criterion — it is a placeholder note describing a current gap rather than a specified deliverable.
findings.location: What exists vs. what we build — Net-new bullet: 'Routing derivation/validation pipeline'
findings.suggested_fix: If out-of-scope, move it to a 'Future work' section. If in-scope, add an AC such as: '`rebar criteria validate-routing` reports schema violations and unreferenced criterion ids in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`, exit-nonzero on any violation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the mtime option from ef7e's What section so the cache-key spec is unambiguous: content-hash (sha256) only, consistent with the Remediation section and parent design.
findings.finding: ef7e's 'What' section specifies the overlay cache key as '(repo_root, overlay mtime) — note mtime granularity, prefer content hash if flaky', while the Remediation section definitively mandates content-hash (sha256). This internal contradiction leaves the cache-key implementation strategy ambiguous for an implementer reading the What section, potentially producing mtime-based caching that is coarser and cross-repo-leak-prone in rapid-edit scenarios.
findings.location: child ef7e-c3ed-296b-4642
findings.suggested_fix: In ef7e's What section, replace '(uncached, or keyed by (repo_root, overlay mtime) — note mtime granularity, prefer content hash if flaky)' with '(keyed by (repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes)) — content signature, not mtime)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how credentials are supplied to the `POST /criterion/preview` endpoint (e.g., 'same env-var set as the existing `agents` extra; absent credentials → 503 with a human-readable error') to eliminate the implicit auth assumption.
findings.finding: The editor live-preview endpoint's authentication/credential requirements are only loosely described ('behind the `agents` extra + credentials') with no specification of what credentials are required, how they are supplied, or what happens when credentials are absent — leaving an implementing agent to choose an appropriate auth mechanism.
findings.location: What exists vs. what we build — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence: 'Credentials are resolved via the same mechanism as the existing `agents` extra (e.g., `REBAR_LLM_API_KEY`); if absent, the endpoint returns HTTP 503 with body `{"error": "credentials_required"}` and does not fall back to async.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify an expand-contract sequence for `drift_refresh_candidate` and `remediation_mode_candidate` signature changes (e.g., `repo_root=None` default preserving today's behavior when absent) so callers can migrate incrementally, or document a rollback path if the signature change must be atomic.
findings.finding: `drift_refresh_candidate` and `remediation_mode_candidate` are called out as needing `repo_root` threaded through them, but the plan provides no expand-contract sequence or rollback path for these specific function-signature changes — only a general 'registry_version is expand-contract' statement that does not cover the callsite signature breakage if upstream callers (CLI, workflow) cannot supply `repo_root`.
findings.location: § Attestation & invalidation — 'thread `repo_root` through every caller — `claim_gate_check → compute_validity`, and also `drift_refresh_candidate` and `remediation_mode_candidate`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `repo_root=None` as a default parameter to both functions: when `None`, call `registry_version()` as today (no-op change for existing callers); when provided, call `registry_version(repo_root)`. This is a pure expand-contract addition — existing callers are unaffected and callers can adopt `repo_root` one by one.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify the sync-first choice over async job+poll for `POST /criterion/preview` given the 60s timeout risk, OR invert the default (ship async job+poll first since it is the established pattern for long-running HTTP ops) and confirm `ThreadingHTTPServer`'s threading behavior is sufficient for the expected concurrent-preview load.
findings.finding: The editor live-preview design proposes a synchronous `POST /criterion/preview` with a 'configurable timeout (default 60s)' on the existing `ThreadingHTTPServer`, with async job+poll as a fallback only 'if a single sync preview does not return within the timeout on the reference model.' This chooses sync-first without considering the established prior art that async-first (e.g., an asyncio-based server or a background worker queue) is the standard solution for long-running operations on HTTP servers, and does not justify why sync-first is preferred given the known 60s blocking risk to the single-threaded handler while other editor requests queue.
findings.location: § 'What exists vs. what we build' → 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview' bullet ('Spike gate: if a single sync preview does not return within the timeout on the reference model, fall back to an async job+poll shape rather than blocking the editor's `ThreadingHTTPServer` handler')

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
